title: The One Where Eddie Moves In

lines.text: Originally written by Adam Chase.
Joey's new apartment. Everyone but Chandler is there. Joey has decorated the place with tons of tacky stuff.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh? So whaddya think? Casa de Joey. Huh? I decorated it myself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get out.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: [looking at some kind of glass sculpture thing] Wow Joey, this is, uhh...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Art.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Art it is.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [looking at a glass table with a panther shaped base] Look, check this out. Is it a coffee table, is it a panther? There's no need to decide.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: [holding a pillow made out of 4 inch red fur] Hey, nice pillow. So now tell me, is this genuine Muppet skin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [looking at a water sculpture that looks like a window with rain running down it] Hey, excellent, excellent water-table thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks, yeah. I love this but ya know what, it makes me wanna pee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well me too, yeah. I think that's the challenge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, how come, uhh, Chandler didn't come?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well uh, it's cause he had a thing with, wi-, with the thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right, I go-, I got it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So why don't ya show us the rest of your casa?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Uh, oh, OH, the best part, c'mon. [leads them to the bathroom, gestures towards toilet, everyone stares, uncomprehending] Heh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, nice toilet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No no no, behind it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha-, you have a phone in here?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right, I have a phone in here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, promise me something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Never call me from that phone.
OPENING TITLES
Central Perk. Monica, Chandler, and Ross are seated. Rachel is walking over with coffee and a piece of pie.
[Someone bumps into Rachel and she drops the pie in a guy's hood that's seated at the table. She improvises by using the plate as a saucer for the coffee.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, here we go. Honey, I'm sorry, they were all out of apple pie, someone just got the last piece.
[Phoebe enters]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, oh my God, oh my God, oh my God. You are not gonna believe this. I have just been discovered.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now wait a minute, I claimed you in the name of France four years ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Anyway, OK, now promise you won't like, freak out and say how great this is until I'm done, OK.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. I just met this producer of this like, teeny record company, who said that I have a very fresh, offbeat sound and she wants to do a demo of Smelly Cat.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: [congradulating her and celebrating]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I told you not to do that yet. And, she wants to do a video.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: [celebrating more]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm not done yet, OK. God. OK, if that goes well, they may even want to make an album.
[everyone is quiet, unsure if she's done or not]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm done now.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: [celebrating]
[the guy with the pie in his hood get up to leave]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God. Ross, OK, if you care about me at all, you will get the pie out of the man's hood.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Get the what?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pie in the hood, pie in the hood. Go.
[Ross goes over behind the guy and grabs the pie out of his hood as he leaves]

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: What're you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry, my pie was, was in your hood. Now I just have to get the coffee out of that guy's pants and I'll be back in the hospital by 7. [swats at an imaginary insect by his head, guy leaves promptly]
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica is outside the bathroom yelling at Ross who's in the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Damnit Ross, get your butt out of the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Calm down, I'm blow drying.
[Rachel enters with laundry and starts folding]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Blow drying what, you have no hair.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's goin' on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Your boyfriend has been in there for over an hour. I can't believe it, it's like I'm living with him again. He's here when I go to sleep, he's here when I wake up, he's here when I want to use the shower, ughh. It's like I'm sixteen all over again .

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, you're not sixteen, you're both adults now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: GET OUT YOU DUFUS!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Or ya know, he's rubber and you're glue.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [comes out] All yours.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I hope you cleaned your hair out of the drain.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [in a childish voice] I hope you cleaned your hair out of the drain.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shut up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [childish voice] Shut up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Cut it out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: [childish voice] Mi-mi-mii.
[Monica goes in the bathroom]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: [sarcastically] I've never wanted you more.
Chandler's apartment. Chandler is sitting on the bar wearing huge dog-slippers

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, whaddya say boys, should I call him? [squeezes the ear of one of the slippers and it barks] Well, ya know what they say. Ask your slippers a question... you're going crazy.
[Joey's apartment, phone rings]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, I'm, I'm sorry I didn't make it over there today.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that's OK. You uh, you had a thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah well, I hear the place looks great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh, forget about it, I'm havin' a ball. How's the apartment doin'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh hey, it's, it's terriffic. I mean it's a regular space... fest.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, well great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah I just... wanted to call and say hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well OK then. [oven timer goes off behind Chandler] Was that the oven timer?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's right my friend. It's time for...

lines.speaker: Both
lines.text: Baywatch!! [both turn on TV's]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, can you believe they gave Stephanie skin cancer?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I still can't believe they promoted her to lieutenant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Naa, you're just sayin' that 'cause you're in love with Yasmine Blepe.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, how could anyone not be in love with Yasmine Blepe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, they're runnin'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, this is the brilliance of the show. I say always keep them running. All the time, running. Run. Run Yasmine, run like the wind.
Central Perk. Joey is sitting between Monica and Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But I thought you wanted to live by yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I did. I thought it'd be great. I figured I'd have like, time alone with my thoughts but, ya know, it turns out I don't have as many thoughts as you'd think.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, why don't you talk to Chandler about moving back?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You really think he'd take me? I mean, we had a pretty good talk last night but, when I moved out, I hurt him bad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I promise you, he would definitely want you back.
Chandler's apartment. Chandler is sitting between Rachel and Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm telling you, there's no way he's moving back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But we had one of the greatest talks we ever had last night. I mean it was, it was like when we first started living together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I know you don't want to hear this right now but, we've seen him in his new place, alright. And he's happy, he's, he's decorated.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, Chandler, he has moved on, OK, you have to too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. You're just gonna have to accept the fact that you're just friends now, OK, you're not... rommmates anymore.
Recording studio. Phoebe is getting ready to record Smelly Cat.

lines.speaker: Producer
lines.text: OK Phoebe, you ready to try one?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. [singing] Smelly cat, smell-ly cat, what are they feeding you? Smelly cat [back up singers start singing smelly, smelly, smelly, smelly behind her] Oh woah, oh my God. I mean like, who was that?
PRODUCER:They're your backup singers... beind you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OH!! Oh I thought they were just watching me. You know, like at, like at an aquarium, ya know.

lines.speaker: Producer
lines.text: Alrighty. From the top.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: OK. [singing] Smelly cat, smell-ly cat, what are they feeding you? Smelly cat [back up singers - smelly, smelly, smelly, really bad smelly cat, it's not your fault] OK, sorry. I'm just, I'm just not getting that everyone um, gets how smelly this cat acually is. I just think that maybe if we could talk about this, 'cause I need to feel that you really care about the cat.

lines.speaker: Producer
lines.text: Honey, uh we, we can talk about this. It's just that it's costing about a hundred dollars a minute to be in here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh OK. So, um, the cat stinks but you love it, let's go.
Monica and Chandler's apartment. Ross is on the phone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, there is no way he was a velociraptor. No Tony, look at the cranial ridge, OK. If Dino was a velociraptor, he would have eaten the Flintstones. Yeah, yeah. [Monica comes out of her room] Oh, were you takin' a nap?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh I-, Oh wait, Tony can you hang on? That's the other line. [gets the other line] Hello. Oh yeah she's here but uh, can she call you back? OK thanks. [hangs up the other line] Call Joanna. [back on with Tony] Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did she leave a number?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you see me write one down?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't have her number, butt-munch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, she'll call back, don't be such a baby.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm not a baby, you're the baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, you wanna get off my back?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You wanna get out of my face?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait hold on Tony, hold on. [answers second line] Hello. Hi, yeah no, she's right here. Um hold on. [gets first line] Hi Tony, can I call you back? That's uh, that's my sister's boyfriend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Give me that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: OK.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi sweetie, look before I forget, did I leave my diaphram at your place? Hi mom. [she starts throwing oranges at Ross who's looking pleased with himself]
Chandler's apartment. Chandler is sitting on the bar, bouncing a ball against the door. Joey walks in right as he throws the ball and catches it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So uhh, how's the palace?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know it's funny you should mention that 'cause I was thinkin'... what's with the boxes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, uhh, actually I uh, have some news.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Hey Chan, is that Joey guy gonna come by and pick up his moose hat or should I just toss it out?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, uh, why don't you ask him yourself. Joey, this is my new roommate Eddie.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Nice to meet ya.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Likewise. Uh, I'll take that. [grabs moose hat] It's what I came for. So, this is new. Where'd you two meet?

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: At the uh, supermarket, in the uh, ethnic food section. I helped him pick out a chorizo.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you know, we got to talking and uh, he said he needed a place and I had a spare room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, now it's a spare room?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well yeah, in that it's not being used and I... have it to spare.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I uh, got what I came for. [puts on moose hat] I'll uh, I'll see you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Jo. When'd you start usin' mousse in your hair?

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: [annoying laugh] Is this guy great or what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah he is. [leaves]
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Monica, Rachel, and Ross are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe he has a new roommate. Who is this guy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Eddie something. He just met him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It'll never last, he's just a rebound roommate.
[Pheobe enters]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, check it out, oh check it out. It's Smelly Cat the video.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: [cheer]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now OK, I haven't seen it yet so, if you don't like it, well, so what, none of you ever made a video. [puts the tape in] OK.
[The video is a very dramatic episode with an obviously dubbed voice for Phoebe. Everyone watches in disbeliefe]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I sound amazing. I, I, I've never heard myself sing before. I mean, except in my own head. Oh, this is so cool, now I can hear what you hear.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pretty uhm, different huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I am sorry but I am incredibly talented.
Chandler and Eddie's apartment. Joey stops by. Chandler is reading the paper and Eddie is fixing eggs.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Hi Joey, what's goin' on man?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Eddie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Morning. I just uh, came by to pick up my mail. [looks for the mail on the table by the door, it's not there] Where's the mail?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh it's uh, over there on the table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You don't keep it over here on this table any more?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, Eddie likes to keep it over there.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Alright, here you go my friend. Eggs a-la Eddie, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, ooh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No I just uh, thought you liked your eggs with the bread with the hole in the middle, a-la me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I do, but uh, Eddie makes them this way and, well they're pretty darn good.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Well you guys, I'm outta here. See ya pals.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See ya. [Eddie leaves]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So how you two gettin' along?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I couldn't be happier.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great, well, I'm happy for ya. [picks up the orange juice carton and it's empty] Alright that's it. He just comes in here, Mr. Jonny Neweggs, with his, his, his movin' the mail and his, his 'see ya pals'. And now there's no juice. There's no juice f or the people who need the juice and want the juice. I need the juice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's another carton right over there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, this isn't about juice anymore, alright man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright, so what's it about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Eggs. Who's eggs do you like better, his or mine, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well I like both eggs equally.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh come on. Nobody likes two different kinds of eggs equally. You like one better than the other and I wanna know which.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well what's the difference? Your eggs aren't here anymore, are they? You took your eggs and you left. You really expect me to never find new eggs?
Monica and Rachel's apartment. Ross and Monica are fighting over the remote.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wanna watch Entertainment Tonight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tough noogies, we're watching Predators of the Serengetti.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you guys stop.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's my TV.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha-, oh, quit it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bite me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, Monica keeps changin' the channel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that's great, why don't you tell mommy on me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now I'm mommy in this little play? Alright look, I refuse to get sucked into this like, weird little Geller dimension thing OK. So I'm gonna go and take a nice long hot bubble bath because you kids are driving me crazy. [goes in the bathroom]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: OK, what're we gonna do about this?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I guess we could tape Entertainment Tonight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not that, this, US. Oh my God, Ross, you-re, you're, it's jus-, you-, ever sin- you been here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ow, ow, OK. Alright, alright, Mon, Mon, you've gone ultrasonic again, alright.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just can't stand you being here all the time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why, why, why can't you stand me being here? I don't, I, we're just, ya know, we're just havin' fun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fun? Fun, you think this is fun?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, c'mon I mean I though, you know, I thought we're just foolin' around. Like when, uh, when we were kids.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, I hated you when we were kids.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You hated me when we were kids?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes. I hated you. I mean I, I, loved you in a 'you're my brother so I have to' kind of way, but basically, yeah, I hated your guts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why did you hate me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because, you were mean to me and you, you teased me and you always, always got your way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that wasn't fun for you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Duh-huh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe you hated me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now I love you. And not just 'cause I have to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. You're just gonna have to stop pissing me off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then I won't have to kill you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you wanna watch uh, Entertainment Tonight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, thanks. You know what?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: If you really want to watch that Serengetti thing, you can.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh... OK. [changes the channel]
Central Perk. Ross, Rachel, and Monica are hanging out.
[Phoebe enters]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen. You are not going to believe this but, that is not me singing on the video.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, how did you find out?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, OK, the record company sent over this piece of paper for me to sign, saying that it's OK for someone else to sing for me. That was my first clue.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So what're you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I can't work with people who would do this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I mean this poor woman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What woman?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The voice woman. Ya know, I mean, she has a great voice but she doesn't have a video.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OK, Phoebs. But what about you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I have a video, you have to pay attention. No this, this voice woman, she's so talented but, according to the producer people, they said she doesn't have like the right look or something, ya know. I mean, it's like, she's like one of those an imals at the pound who like nobody wants 'cause they're not pretty enough or you know. Like, like some old dog who's just kind of like stinky and. Huuuuh, oh my God, she's smelly cat. Oh, oh that song has so many levels.
Chandler and Eddie's apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Eddie, you uh, wanna play some foosball?

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: No thanks man, I'm not uh, I'm not really into sports.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: [stares in disbeliefe] Yeah o-, OK, alright. [oven timer goes off] Doesn't matter, time for Baywatch.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Y-, y-, you like that show?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't like that show?

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: Wha-, n-, no. I mean it's just a bunch of pretty people runnin' around on the beach, ya know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well that's the brilliance of it. The pretty people... and the running.

lines.speaker: Eddie
lines.text: I tell ya, I-, I'm gonna go read in my room for a little while.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh o-, OK man.
[Joey's place. He's watching Baywatch, lauging. He goes to say something to Chandler in the other chair but no one's there. He goes to call Chandler but decides not to.]
[Chandler's. He's playing foosball by himself.]
[Joey's. Playing ping pong by himself.]

[All by myself is playing. Chandler is sitting in front of a window while it's raining outside. We see Joey through a rainy window. The camera zooms out to show it's just his tabletop water sculpture.]
CLOSING CREDITS
Central Perk. Phoebe is performing Smelly Cat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: [singing] Smelly cat, smell-ly cat, what are they feeding you? Everybody.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Smelly cat, smell-ly cat, it's not your fault.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: [sings] They won't take you to the vet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: [reluctantly sings] You're obviously not their favorite pet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: [sings] It may not be a bed of roses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: [sings] And you're no friend to those with noses.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, Ross, those are the only lines we have, sorry. OK, you guys, once more.
END
